title: Watch how coffee flavor changes.
title2: Move the grind and the ratio.
title3: Advanced Mode for Temperature, Pressure, and Water.
subtitle: An educational heuristic based on extraction theory. Useful to understand whether your recipe will lean more acidic, more bitter, or fuller-bodied.
adjust: Adjust the recipe and type of coffee. The result is updated in real time.
state_desc_sub: You’ll likely get more pronounced acidity and a lighter body. Try grinding finer or slightly increasing the ratio.
state_desc_bal: Sweet spot: more sweetness and clarity in the cup.
state_desc_over: Bitterness or astringency may show up. Try grinding coarser or shortening the ratio.
note: Note: This is an educational heuristic, not a physical measurement.
style_ristretto_desc: Shorter and more concentrated: more intensity and body, less clarity.
style_espresso_desc: Classic balance: a good mix of intensity, sweetness and clarity.
style_lungo_desc: Longer: often more clarity, but it increases the risk of bitterness and astringency.
roastAdvice: Tip: light roasts tend to punish underextraction more, while dark roasts bring bitterness earlier.
controlsDescription: Adjust the recipe and coffee type. The result updates in real time.
mapHelpP1: The horizontal axis represents ratio and the vertical axis represents grind. The dot shows where your recipe lands.
mapHelpP2: Blue indicates underextraction tendency, green the balanced zone, and red overextraction tendency.
mapHelpP3: Finer grind usually moves the recipe toward higher extraction. Coarser grind toward lower extraction. A longer ratio shifts the point to the right.
mapHelpP4: Temperature and pressure act as secondary adjustments and slightly shift the point.
radarHelpP1: The radar summarizes the estimated sensory profile of the recipe across five axes: acidity, sweetness, bitterness, astringency, and body.
radarHelpP2: A higher value does not necessarily mean better: it indicates a stronger presence of that attribute in the cup.
radarHelpP3: Underextracted recipes usually show more acidity and less body. Overextracted ones tend to increase bitterness and astringency.
radarHelpP4: Roast, process, temperature, pressure, and water modify the final profile in addition to grind and ratio.
